scenario.title: Deepen the codebase — catalog module
scenario.question: Where should the catalog architecture deepen next?
scenario.lede: The tag filter shipped; three seams are now visibly shallow. Each direction below is analysis at equal fidelity — dependency diagram, evidence, costs, and the blueprint seed it would hand to change-blueprint. Nothing is refactored here.
inputs: src/catalog.mjs — data + filtering in one module

currentMap.caption: Today: catalog.mjs owns both the data and the filtering; app.mjs owns both wiring and empty-state construction; tests import across two seams.

options.label: Split data from filtering
options.claim: catalog.mjs quietly grew two jobs — the skill data and the filtering logic. Splitting them makes the data swappable without touching behavior.
options.flowCaption: skills-data.mjs becomes a pure data module; filter.mjs depends on nothing but its input shape.
pros: data changes stop rippling into behavior tests
a future remote catalog swaps one module
cons: one more file for a five-skill catalog
options.blueprintSeed: Scope: extract skills-data.mjs; filter.mjs takes skills as input. Non-goal: any behavior change. Criteria: existing assertions pass unchanged against the split.

options.label: Give the empty state a home
options.claim: app.mjs builds the accepted empty state inline — markup, chips, and copy in glue code. Extracting empty-state.mjs makes the accepted design a named, tested artifact.
options.flowCaption: empty-state.mjs renders from the same data island the catalog uses; app.mjs shrinks to pure wiring.
pros: the accepted empty state becomes directly testable
glue shrinks to wiring, its stated job
chip drift from real tags becomes a test failure
cons: touches the code most recently stabilized
empty-state markup in JS needs the same no-sink discipline
options.blueprintSeed: Scope: extract emptyState(tag) into empty-state.mjs with direct tests asserting chips == real tag set. Non-goal: visual changes. Criteria: unknown-tag criterion 3 passes against the extracted module.

options.label: Fold url-tag into catalog
options.claim: Two modules for forty lines may be one seam too many — folding parseTagFromSearch into catalog.mjs trades a boundary for simplicity.
options.flowCaption: One module owns tag semantics end to end; app.mjs imports a single surface.
cons: re-couples URL parsing to data — the seam three-code-paths deliberately created
URL semantics lose their own test file
swims against the accepted dependency direction
options.blueprintSeed: Scope: merge url-tag.mjs into catalog.mjs preserving both export names. Non-goal: signature changes. Criteria: all existing assertions pass from the single module.

recommendation.reason: It is the only direction that turns an accepted decision (the empty state) into tested, named code — the other two move boxes without changing what can fail. The seed is small, the criteria are mechanical, and it reduces the file most likely to grow badly.

unresolved: direction A becomes attractive the moment a second catalog source appears

accepted.acceptedVia: chat 2026-07-23 — candidate pasted and confirmed after comparing the three dependency diagrams
accepted.recordTitle: Catalog deepening: give the empty state a home (direction B)
accepted.summary: Direction B accepted: extract the accepted empty state from app.mjs glue into a named, directly tested empty-state.mjs. Its change-blueprint seed carries forward; directions A and C are preserved with their reopening conditions.

decisions.rationale: The empty state is the one accepted design living as untested glue; naming it turns chip drift into a test failure.

exampleWorking.rationale: The empty state is the one accepted design living as untested glue; naming it turns chip drift into a test failure.

accepted.canonicalBody:
# Catalog deepening — accepted direction

Direction B: extract emptyState(tag) into empty-state.mjs with direct
tests asserting the chip set equals the real tag set. Blueprint seed
handed to change-blueprint. Directions A (data/behavior split) and C
(fold url-tag) preserved with change conditions.
